label: College Student

segment.role: budget-constrained student balancing academics and social life
segment.context: Living on limited funds while needing reliable products for school and daily life. Prioritizes value but won't compromise on core functionality.

goals.goal: Find affordable but durable products that won't break before semester ends
Avoid products with compatibility issues with existing devices
Verify product quality matches description before purchasing
Quickly identify if product has known defects or common failure points
Compare alternatives when primary choice has quality concerns

constraints: No time for complicated returns/refunds process
Small living space - can't accommodate bulky items
Need products that work immediately without extensive setup
behavioral_rules: Immediately checks '1-star' reviews for consistent failure patterns
Abandons page if product description lacks specific durability claims
Compares prices across 3 tabs before considering purchase
Spends extra time verifying compatibility with existing devices
Searches for '[product name] broke' before final decision
Prefers products with clear warranty/return policy near add-to-cart button
Skips products requiring account creation to see pricing
Prioritizes products with video demonstrations of actual use
Returns to comparison page 2-3 times before finalizing choice
Immediately exits if checkout process has more than 3 steps
deal_breakers: Shipping costs exceed 10% of product price
voice_sample: I just need this graphing calculator to last through finals without glitching. The TI-84 has better reviews than the off-brand ones, but why does it cost so much? Three reviews said the buttons stop working after a month - that's exactly what happened to my last one. If I'm spending this much, it better not have the same cheap parts. Maybe I should check the refurbished options... but what if those are just returns from people who got defective units? Ugh, why is everything either crazy expensive or breaks immediately?

browsing_patterns.primary_device: Laptop during daytime, phone at night

purchase_journey.discovery: Starts with Amazon search or direct Google search for specific product needs
purchase_journey.evaluation: Checks price first, then immediately sorts reviews by lowest rating to identify failure patterns
purchase_journey.decision_moment: When finding a product with <10% 1-star reviews mentioning durability issues
purchase_journey.post_purchase_expectation: Immediate email confirmation with clear return policy details

converts_when: Sees 'Amazon's Choice' badge on affordable option
Finds detailed negative review with manufacturer response
Product video shows actual college student using item
Price is at least 15% below campus bookstore
abandons_when: Sees 'product arrives defective' in multiple recent reviews
trusts_when: Clear warranty terms in product description
Multiple photos of actual product (not renders)
becomes_suspicious_when: Price seems too good to be true

inner_monologue_samples: <Thought at pricing page: '$129? That's half my textbook budget for the month... but if it lasts 4 years maybe it's worth it'>
<Thought when they see social proof: 'These 5-star reviews all sound like they were written the same day... fake?'>
<Thought when navigation is confusing: 'Why can't I just see all the graphing calculators at once? I don't want to click through 5 categories'>
<Thought at signup form: 'They want my student ID just to see the price? Yeah right'>
<Thought when they find what they're looking for: 'Finally - one with actual photos from students and a review that says it survived being dropped'>
anti_patterns: Assuming they'll pay premium for 'student-oriented' branding
Hiding shipping costs until final checkout step
Requiring account creation to compare products

traits.label: Product Reliability & Quality Concern
traits.description: A focus on the failure of products to meet basic expectations for durability, proper functionality upon arrival, and ease of assembly, leading to frustration over wasted money and time.
key_phrases: product breaks quickly
Products that are cheaply made, poor quality, or non-functional upon arrival
product arrives defective or missing parts

traits.label: Reliability & Fit Frustration
traits.description: Users are primarily frustrated when replacement parts or accessories fail to work reliably, break quickly, or do not fit properly with their existing appliances, causing inconvenience and additional expense.
key_phrases: Products breaking or wearing out quickly (lack of durability)
Products not fitting correctly (size/compatibility issues)
Poor quality compared to original part

traits.label: Practical Usability Concern
traits.description: Reviews emphasize frustration when essential baby products fail at their core functional tasks, causing inconvenience, mess, or discomfort. Parents have low tolerance for logistical difficulties, physical discomfort for their child, or safety worries, prioritizing reliable performance that simplifies caregiving.
key_phrases: Difficulty with product setup/assembly
Child discomfort due to hard or uncomfortable materials
Products that are bulky, heavy, or not portable
Leaks and blowouts causing mess and inconvenience
Skin irritation, rashes, or allergic reactions from products

traits.label: Gameplay Quality
traits.description: Focuses on the technical execution, narrative engagement, and design balance of gameplay, specifically how issues like bugs, poor story, or repetitive tasks detract from an immersive and rewarding experience.
key_phrases: Repetitive or tedious gameplay puzzles
Unengaging or weak storylines
Poor technical performance (bugs, crashes, lag)

traits.label: Technical Frustration
traits.description: Frustration with tech products that are difficult to set up, incompatible with other devices, unreliable, or poorly documented. Users want seamless integration and intuitive operation without constant troubleshooting.
key_phrases: Frustration with complex setup/configuration
Incompatibility issues between devices/platforms
Products lacking clear instructions/setup guidance

traits.label: Reliable & Effortless Outdoor Solutions
traits.description: Users seek products that are consistently effective, durable, and easy to use for garden and outdoor tasks, minimizing physical strain and ensuring safety.
key_phrases: Difficulty with kneeling due to physical limitations
Frustration with ineffective or harmful products
Effective pest control without harming pets/environment

traits.label: Quality Control & Material Sufficiency
traits.description: Reviews in this cluster center on frustration with materials that are defective, insufficient, or visually misrepresented, preventing the successful and satisfying completion of craft projects. Crafters feel misled by poor quality and shortages that ruin their creative experience.
key_phrases: Poor product quality (grainy pigments, cheap appearance, light weight)
Running out of materials (drills, beads, yarn) before project completion
Inaccurate color representation online vs. in person

traits.label: Reliability and Quality Assurance
traits.description: This trait represents a strong consumer focus on receiving products that work correctly and consistently from the start, with durable construction that matches advertised expectations. Users are frustrated by defects, premature failures, and performance inconsistencies that disrupt their core tasks.
key_phrases: Defective or damaged items upon arrival
Products that do not perform core functions
Poor product quality or defects upon arrival
Inconsistent ink flow or pen performance
Breaks or fails quickly

traits.label: Quality Disappointment
traits.description: Customers are deeply frustrated when products fail to meet advertised quality standards, feeling cheated by mismatched descriptions and subpar construction that undermines trust in the brand.
key_phrases: Low-quality materials that feel cheap or flimsy
Product doesn't match description (e.g., font style, performance claims)
Poor quality construction (e.g., seams, glue, weak materials)

traits.label: Balanced Evaluator
traits.description: Customers who weigh both positive and negative aspects of a product carefully before deciding to purchase, showing neither immediate enthusiasm nor outright rejection.

issues: Very few negative reviews — pain points may be underrepresented
Single source — add Reddit or CSV for richer behavioral signal
